message:
fix(crypto): migrate HKDF + HMAC-SHA256 to ring and pin aes-gcm/sha2/hkdf majors

Pinning and unification to stop a recurring "works on my machine"
build break: with wildcard deps, cargo would happily resolve hkdf
0.13 / hmac 0.13 (digest 0.11 family) while aes-gcm 0.10 still
imports KeyInit from digest 0.10, producing ~29 trait-bound errors in
src/encryption.rs.

Cargo.toml
- Pin aes-gcm = "0.10", sha2 = "0.10", hkdf = "0.12" so the digest
  0.10 family (and its KeyInit trait) stays consistent end-to-end.
- Comment explains the constraint so a future refactor doesn't
  silently relax these back to "*".

src/encryption.rs
- Replace hkdf + sha2 with ring's hkdf + hmac primitives. Wraps
  hkdf_extract_expand() around Salt::new(HKDF_SHA256, salt).extract
  → prk.expand → okm.fill, using a local KeyType impl
  (HkdfOutputLen) to carry the requested output length into ring's
  static-length API.
- hkdf_extract() now uses ring's hmac::Key::new(HMAC_SHA256, salt)
  and hmac::sign(..) to produce the 32-byte PRK directly.

src/pages/attachments.rs, src/pages/encrypt.rs, src/pages/key_management.rs
- Drop the duplicated `use hkdf::Hkdf; use sha2::Sha256;` plus the
  inline Hkdf::<Sha256>::new(..).expand(..) pattern in each
  derive_blob_nonce / derive_kek_hkdf call site.
- Route every HKDF-expand through the canonical
  crate::encryption::hkdf_extract_expand(...) helper and coerce the
  returned Vec<u8> to the expected fixed-size output via try_into().

Behavior is byte-identical (ring's HKDF-SHA256 and the rust-crypto
hkdf crate produce the same PRK/OKM for a given salt/ikm/info/len),
and the "HKDF should never fail for N bytes" expectations are
preserved on the panic path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -79,14 +79,19 @@ bloomfilter = "*"  # Probabilistic membership testing for P2 Opt 3.3: Workspace
 hnsw_rs = "*"  # Opt 9: Approximate Nearest Neighbor with HNSW
 ouroboros = "*"  # Safe self-referential wrapper for HNSW loader lifetime
 
-# Crypto for ChatGPT encrypted conversations and HTML export encryption
-aes-gcm = "*"
+# Crypto for ChatGPT encrypted conversations and HTML export encryption.
+# The aes-gcm + digest + hkdf + hmac + sha2 family must stay on compatible
+# majors: aes-gcm 0.10 uses digest 0.10, which is what sha2 0.10 / hmac 0.12 /
+# hkdf 0.12 export. Leaving these as wildcards lets cargo upgrade hkdf + hmac
+# to 0.13 (digest 0.11), which fails to satisfy aes-gcm's KeyInit bound and
+# breaks the encryption.rs build with ~29 trait-bound errors.
+aes-gcm = "0.10"
 ring = "*"
 url = "*"
 pbkdf2 = "*"  # PBKDF2 key derivation for Web Crypto compatible HTML export encryption
 
 # SHA256 hashing for model verification
-sha2 = "*"
+sha2 = "0.10"
 hex = "*"
 
 # Interactive terminal prompts for setup wizard
@@ -97,7 +102,7 @@ ssh2 = "*"
 # Statically link OpenSSL so release binaries don't require system libssl.so.3
 openssl = { version = "*", features = ["vendored"] }
 argon2 = "*"
-hkdf = "*"
+hkdf = "0.12"  # Must match hmac 0.12 / sha2 0.10 / aes-gcm 0.10 digest family; see comment above.
 zeroize = { version = "*", features = ["derive"] }
 flate2 = "*"
 rand = "0.10"
